Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED default false, EMAIL_PROVIDER default disabled, EMAIL_DELIVERY_EVENTS_ENABLED default false, EMAIL_INBOUND_ENABLED default false.
Acceptance Criteria: Settings load in dev/test/prod and all Email settings are disabled by default.
Expected Evidence: Test output plus env matrix diff.
Rollback Impact: Unset new envs returns system to pre-email behavior.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Only allow EMAIL_PROVIDER in disabled/ses. Invalid provider must fail startup in production and test.
Acceptance Criteria: Invalid EMAIL_PROVIDER raises RuntimeError with actionable message.
Rollback Impact: Revert validation block if needed; defaults remain disabled.

Atomic Change: Separate Email runtime gate from OUTBOUND_PROVIDER
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Create email-specific gate using ENABLE_OUTBOUND_DISPATCH + OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED + EMAIL_PROVIDER; do not use OUTBOUND_PROVIDER=ses.
Acceptance Criteria: Email can be enabled while OUTBOUND_PROVIDER remains openclaw; OUTBOUND_PROVIDER=ses is not required or accepted.
Expected Evidence: Test proves WhatsApp provider setting remains openclaw.
Rollback Impact: Disable OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED to stop Email only.

Atomic Change: Add production validation for Email secret references
Exact Code-Level Requirement: When OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED=true and EMAIL_PROVIDER=ses in production, require EMAIL_SES_SECRET_REF or AWS runtime credentials strategy; never require raw secrets in DB.
Acceptance Criteria: Production startup fails with missing credential strategy; dev/test can run with fake provider.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Validate EMAIL_SES_REGION when EMAIL_PROVIDER=ses; expose explicit config for receiving region when inbound is enabled.
Acceptance Criteria: Invalid/missing SES region blocks Email-enabled startup.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add email_channel_accounts table linked 1:1 to channel_accounts with provider, from_email, from_name, reply_to_email, return_path_email, region, configuration_set, secret_ref, is_verified, health_status fields.

Rollback Impact: Alembic downgrade removes table without touching existing channel_accounts.

Atomic Change: Add uniqueness constraints for Email account identity
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Enforce one EmailChannelAccount per channel_account_id and unique active from_email+market scope where applicable.
Acceptance Criteria: Duplicate active email sender scope is rejected.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add table storing outbound_message_id, subject, to_email, cc_json, bcc_json, from_email, reply_to_email, message_id_header, in_reply_to, references_json, provider_request_json, provider_response_json.
Acceptance Criteria: Metadata row can be created and linked to TicketOutboundMessage.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Index outbound_message_id unique, provider_message_id, message_id_header, to_email, created_at.
Acceptance Criteria: Query plan/metadata inspector confirms indexes exist.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add table for provider, provider_event_id, provider_message_id, event_type, recipient_email, raw_payload_json, occurred_at, outbound_message_id.
Acceptance Criteria: Delivery event can be inserted and linked to outbound message.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Unique provider+provider_event_id or fallback provider+provider_message_id+event_type+recipient_email+occurred_at hash.
Acceptance Criteria: Duplicate webhook event is ignored/rejected deterministically.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add email_suppression_entries table with email_normalized, reason, source_event_id, active, created_at.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Only one active suppression per normalized email+reason class.
Acceptance Criteria: Duplicate active complaint suppression is idempotent.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add inbound email table with message_id_header, from_email, to_email, subject, text_body, html_body_sanitized, raw_storage_key, ticket_id, dedupe_hash, status.
Acceptance Criteria: Inbound email can be stored and linked or marked unresolved.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Unique dedupe_hash for inbound raw message; duplicate inbound does not create duplicate ticket timeline entries.
Acceptance Criteria: Duplicate inbound payload is idempotent.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Implement resolve_channel_account_for_provider(db, provider, market_id, account_id) filtering by provider and active state.
Acceptance Criteria: Resolver never returns a row from a different provider.
Rollback Impact: Revert new resolver only if no Email code uses it.

Atomic Change: Add market/global fallback order to provider-scoped resolver
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Resolution order: explicit account_id, ticket.channel_account_id if same provider, market-scoped active account, global active account, fallback if configured.
Acceptance Criteria: Market account wins over global; wrong provider never wins.

Atomic Change: Patch legacy resolver callsites away from unscoped fallback where unsafe
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Do not let non-email OpenClaw dispatch use email accounts; provider-specific resolution is mandatory where channel is known.
Acceptance Criteria: WhatsApp/Telegram/SMS tests prove email account is ignored.
Rollback Impact: Can revert callsite patch if tests fail before Email enabled.

Atomic Change: Add admin email account list endpoint
Exact Code-Level Requirement: GET /api/admin/email/accounts returns Email account rows with channel account, market, readiness, verification, health.

Atomic Change: Add admin email account create endpoint
Exact Code-Level Requirement: POST /api/admin/email/accounts creates ChannelAccount(provider=email) plus EmailChannelAccount in one transaction; secret_ref only.
Acceptance Criteria: Create returns account and no raw secret is persisted.

Atomic Change: Add create validation for from/reply/return-path emails
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Validate EmailStr and reject CRLF/header injection in all email account fields.
Acceptance Criteria: CRLF payload receives 400.

Atomic Change: Add admin email account update endpoint
Exact Code-Level Requirement: PATCH /api/admin/email/accounts/{id} updates mutable metadata; provider/account identity changes are explicit and audited.
Acceptance Criteria: Patch updates display/from/reply/priority without breaking ChannelAccount.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: POST /activate and /deactivate toggle ChannelAccount.is_active and write admin audit.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: GET /api/admin/email/accounts/{id}/readiness returns missing runtime/account/verification/secret/region/configuration_set items.

Atomic Change: Add SES identity verification refresh endpoint
Exact Code-Level Requirement: POST /verification-check calls provider abstraction and updates is_verified/health fields without exposing secrets.

Atomic Change: Add email account health check endpoint
Exact Code-Level Requirement: POST /health-check validates runtime config, credentials availability, SES send quota/get identity where possible, and persists health_status.
Acceptance Criteria: Healthy/degraded/offline states are deterministic under fake provider.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: POST /test-send sends a controlled test email only to explicitly provided test recipient and marks result as test, not ticket outbound.
Acceptance Criteria: Test send returns provider_message_id under fake SES and rejects missing confirmation.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: GET /api/admin/email/suppressions lists active suppression entries with reason and source event.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: POST /api/admin/email/suppressions/{id}/deactivate requires admin note and writes admin audit.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Add optional subject, to_email, cc, bcc, html_body, attachment_ids fields; existing channel/body requests remain valid.
Acceptance Criteria: Legacy WhatsApp/WebChat payload still validates.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: When channel=email validate recipient, subject generation, cc/bcc format, CRLF rejection, body presence.
Acceptance Criteria: Invalid email payload returns structured validation error.

Atomic Change: Upgrade capability registry to support Email ready/configurable
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Email is customer_sendable only when runtime, active verified account, valid recipient, secret strategy, and no suppression are all ready.
Acceptance Criteria: Email capability returns ready only for fully configured fixture.
Rollback Impact: Email can be forced experimental_not_ready by disabling OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Missing list must include exact tokens: outbound_email_enabled, email_channel_account, verified_email_identity, valid_email_recipient, email_provider_secret, not_suppressed.
Acceptance Criteria: Each missing scenario returns one deterministic token.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: send_outbound_message creates TicketOutboundMessage plus EmailOutboundMetadata in same transaction when channel=email.
Acceptance Criteria: Email send API returns queued row and metadata exists.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: If subject absent, generate Re: [ticket_no] title; preserve existing email thread subject when metadata exists.
Acceptance Criteria: Default subject deterministic and sanitized.

Atomic Change: Resolve Email recipient from explicit/to preferred/customer
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Resolution order: explicit to_email, ticket.preferred_reply_contact, customer.email. Reject source_chat_id unless it is valid email.
Acceptance Criteria: Recipient resolution matrix passes.

Atomic Change: Block suppressed Email recipient before queueing
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Active suppression blocks queueing with structured error; no outbound row is created.
Acceptance Criteria: Suppressed recipient returns 400 and no row.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: claim_pending_messages must claim email only when Email gate is enabled; WhatsApp/Telegram/SMS remain controlled by openclaw gate.
Acceptance Criteria: Email disabled leaves pending email untouched and does not increment retry_count.

Atomic Change: Route Email messages to Email adapter branch
Exact Code-Level Requirement: process_outbound_message handles SourceChannel.email before legacy OpenClaw branch.
Acceptance Criteria: Email message never calls dispatch_via_openclaw_bridge.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Resolve active verified email account, recipient, subject, sender, reply-to, return-path and metadata row.
Acceptance Criteria: Missing account/recipient/metadata returns non-provider failure.
Rollback Impact: No external call if resolution fails.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Generate text and sanitized HTML body; reject script/event handlers and dangerous HTML.
Acceptance Criteria: HTML sanitizer strips unsafe content and text fallback exists.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Generate Message-ID, References, In-Reply-To, X-NexusDesk-Ticket-ID; reject CRLF.
Acceptance Criteria: Headers are deterministic, safe, and stored in metadata.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Define EmailSendResult with status, provider_status, provider_message_id, sent_at, raw_response, retryable flag.
Acceptance Criteria: Fake provider returns typed result consumed by adapter.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Use boto3 SES v2 or SES client to send single-recipient transactional email with source/destination/content/configuration_set.
Acceptance Criteria: Provider builds expected SES request under botocore Stubber/fake.

Atomic Change: Map SES transient errors to retryable
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Throttle, service unavailable, timeout map to retryable failure; no hard dead on first transient.
Acceptance Criteria: Transient error schedules retry.

Atomic Change: Map SES permanent errors to dead
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Invalid identity/suppressed/rejected recipient maps to permanent failure/dead or suppression reason.
Acceptance Criteria: Permanent SES error marks dead with precise failure_code.

Atomic Change: Persist provider message id on send success
Exact Code-Level Requirement: On SES success write provider_message_id to TicketOutboundMessage and EmailOutboundMetadata; mark sent with provider_status.
Acceptance Criteria: Sent row has provider_message_id and sent_at.

Atomic Change: Write outbound_sent ticket event with safe route payload
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Ticket event payload must include email route summary but not raw secrets or full sensitive headers.
Acceptance Criteria: Event exists and does not contain secret_ref value or AWS credentials.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: With OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED=false, pending email remains pending/paused, retry_count unchanged, non-email external dispatch can continue.
Acceptance Criteria: Rollback invariant test passes.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Register /api/integrations/email/events/ses and inbound endpoints behind explicit settings.
Acceptance Criteria: Disabled routes reject or no-op according to settings.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Verify AWS SNS signature or HMAC timestamp anti-replay; hidden URL is not sufficient.
Acceptance Criteria: Unsigned or stale webhook rejected.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Reject repeated event IDs or stale timestamps with no duplicate delivery event.
Acceptance Criteria: Replay attempt creates no duplicate row.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Normalize SES send event to EmailDeliveryEvent(event_type=send) and link by provider_message_id.
Acceptance Criteria: Send event creates delivery event and ticket timeline entry.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Normalize delivery event and update metadata last_delivery_status=delivered.
Acceptance Criteria: Delivery event appears in ticket timeline.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Bounce event records delivery event and creates suppression for hard/permanent bounce.
Acceptance Criteria: Hard bounce suppresses recipient and future send is blocked.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Complaint event records delivery event and creates complaint suppression.
Acceptance Criteria: Complaint suppression blocks future sends.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Reject maps to failed/dead signal; delivery delay maps to delayed provider status without suppression.
Acceptance Criteria: Reject/delay state transitions are deterministic.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Unified timeline includes email queued, accepted/sent, delivered, bounced, complained, delayed.
Acceptance Criteria: Timeline API returns ordered email events.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Parse raw inbound payload into from/to/subject/text/html/message-id/in-reply-to/references and sanitized body.
Acceptance Criteria: Parser extracts core fields from fixture.

Atomic Change: Sanitize inbound HTML and strip quoted dangerous content
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Inbound HTML stored only after sanitization; raw email stored separately if enabled.
Acceptance Criteria: Unsafe inbound HTML is sanitized.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: support+ticket-{ticket_id}@domain deterministically links to existing ticket if visible/open enough.
Acceptance Criteria: Plus address links exact ticket.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Header X-NexusDesk-Ticket-ID links when valid and message passes sender policy.
Acceptance Criteria: Header links ticket.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Headers match previously stored EmailOutboundMetadata message_id_header to link ticket.
Acceptance Criteria: References link ticket.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Subject similarity must never auto-link; unresolved/manual review only.
Acceptance Criteria: Subject-only fixture becomes unresolved.

Atomic Change: Create inbound message ticket comment/timeline item
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Linked inbound customer email updates last_customer_message and timeline without exposing unsafe HTML.
Acceptance Criteria: Linked inbound shows in timeline and reopens waiting_customer ticket.
Rollback Impact: Can delete inbound row in test.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Unlinked inbound emails are stored with status unresolved and exposed via admin endpoint.
Acceptance Criteria: Unresolved inbound can be listed by admin.

Atomic Change: Add manual link endpoint for unresolved inbound
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Admin can link unresolved inbound email to a ticket with audit note.
Acceptance Criteria: Manual link changes status and timeline.
Rollback Impact: Manual unlink endpoint optional; can revert DB row.

Atomic Change: Add Email provider option to account management safely
Exact Code-Level Requirement: Expose Email as separate provider path without breaking WhatsApp/Telegram/SMS form behavior.
Acceptance Criteria: Existing account page still renders existing providers; Email tab appears.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Display from_email, market, provider, verification, health, readiness, active status.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Form collects display name, market, from_email, from_name, reply_to, return_path, region, configuration_set, secret_ref, priority.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Edit mutable fields, active state, fallback, and priority; identity changes clearly warned.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Show missing readiness items from API with novice-friendly copy.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Button calls verification endpoint; displays pending/verified/failed result.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Button calls health-check endpoint and displays healthy/degraded/offline with explanation.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Modal requires test recipient and explicit confirmation; displays provider_message_id/result.
Acceptance Criteria: Test-send modal blocks empty recipient and renders success.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Display suppressed emails with reason/source and deactivate action with note.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: OutboundSendPayload supports optional subject, to_email, cc, bcc, html_body, attachment_ids without breaking legacy sends.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: When selected channel=email, render From/To/Subject fields and readiness warning; non-email UI unchanged.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Map missing tokens to operator-readable messages; disable send button.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Display queued/sent/delivered/bounced/complained/delayed/inbound reply with safe labels.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Display resolved customer email from capability or ticket; show missing customer email guidance.

Exact Code-Level Requirement: Document SES identity, DKIM/SPF/DMARC, region receiving support, SNS/S3 inbound path, secret_ref setup.
